---
title: Colophon
aliases:
  - ../colophon.html
  - ../r/colophon.html
  - ../python/colophon.html
engine: knitr
---
```{python}
#| label: colophon-setup
#| echo: false
exec(open("assets/render-settings.py").read())
```

In this appendix chapter, we provide details on the package versions used in this edition. We rendered the book with `quarto` [@quarto] and host it via GitHub Pages; the complete source is [available from GitHub](https://github.com/tidy-finance/website).\index{Github} \index{Quarto} \index{Colophon} Select your language below to see the corresponding package versions.

::: {.panel-tabset group="language"}
### R

This version of the book was built with R [@R-base] version `{r} paste0(version$major,".", version$minor, " (",version$year, "-", version$month, "-", version$day,", ", version$nickname,")")` and the following packages. We generated all R plots using `ggplot2` and its classic dark-on-light theme (`theme_bw()`).\index{ggplot2 theme}

```{r}
#| label: colophon-package-versions
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(ggplot2) # Packages are not needed but loaded to show up in the colophon
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
dependencies <- renv::dependencies(progress = FALSE) |> count(Package)
packages <- jsonlite::fromJSON("renv.lock", flatten = TRUE)
do.call("rbind", packages$Packages) |>
  as_tibble() |>
  select(Package, Version) |>
  unnest(cols = c("Package", "Version")) |>
  inner_join(dependencies, join_by("Package")) |>
  arrange(Package) |>
  select(-n) |>
  kableExtra::kbl(booktabs = TRUE) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = TRUE)
```

### Python

The book was built with Python [@python] version 3.10.11 and the following packages:

```{python}
#| label: colophon-package-versions-py
#| echo: false
import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT

try:
    import tomllib
except ImportError:
    import tomli as tomllib

def parse_uv_lock(file_path):
    """Parse a uv.lock file and return a list of (package, version) tuples."""
    with open(file_path, 'rb') as file:
        data = tomllib.load(file)

    parsed = []
    for package in data.get('package', []):
        name = package.get('name', '')
        version = package.get('version', '')
        if name:
            parsed.append((name, version))

    return parsed

def to_dataframe(data):
    """Convert parsed data to a polars DataFrame."""
    df = pl.DataFrame(data, schema=["Package", "Version"], orient="row")
    return df

data = parse_uv_lock("uv.lock")
df = to_dataframe(data)
GT(df)
```

:::
